metadata.dagrun:why_previous_looked_the_same: Mapbox vector land is always solid fills unless you add fill-pattern, hillshade, or raster. Earlier JSON only swapped hex colors, so Studio still looked like a normal map.
metadata.dagrun:ascii_in_mapbox: Not supported. Map styles cannot render text/ASCII as land. Use HTML/Canvas on top of the map, or export images elsewhere.
metadata.dagrun:dots_in_mapbox: Possible but not from colors alone: (1) Mapbox Studio → upload a tiny repeating dot PNG into the style sprite, then on the land layer set paint.fill-pattern to that icon id. (2) Or use a raster tileset that is already halftone/stipple. (3) Or many circle layers from point data (heavy).
metadata.dagrun:this_file_adds: Hillshade from Mapbox Terrain DEM so land reads grainy/relief-like (closer to prompt faint hillshade) — visibly different from flat vector.
metadata.mapbox:description: Cream background, solid ocean, semi-transparent land over hillshade.
